text:
Admission Date:  2000-11-30              Discharge Date:   1994-03-12

Date of Birth:  2009-06-17             Sex:   M

Service: CARDIOTHORACIC

Allergies:
Patient recorded as having No Known Allergies to Drugs

Attending:James
Chief Complaint:
Coronary Artery Disease

Major Surgical or Invasive Procedure:
CABGx3(LIMA->LAD, SVG->OM, ramus) 2000-11-30


History of Present Illness:
Mr. Harrell is a 69 year old gentleman with an 8 year history of
chest pain and dyspnea on exertion whcih resolved with rest. He
underwent a stress test which was positive. A cardiac
catheterization was performed which revealed three vessel
disease. He was subsequently referred to Dr. Ramirez for surgical
revascularization.

Past Medical History:
Hypercholesterolemia
Hypertension
Bilateral total knee replacement

Social History:
Lives with wife. Denies alcohol use. 70 pack year smoking
history.

Family History:
Mother with angina

Physical Exam:
Afebrile, 60 NSR, 126/69  99% room air
GEN: No acute distress
NECK: Supple, No JVD, No bruits
HEART: RRR, no murmur
LUNGS: Clear
ABD: Soft, Nontender, nondistended
EXT: No edema

Pertinent Results:
2000-11-30 11:16AM   GLUCOSE-101 NA+-138 K+-4.7
2016-06-14 05:09AM BLOOD WBC-7.4 RBC-3.06* Hgb-9.3* Hct-27.2*
MCV-89 MCH-30.4 MCHC-34.2 RDW-13.7 Plt Ct-178
2016-06-14 05:09AM BLOOD Glucose-81 UreaN-16 Creat-0.9 Na-138
K-3.9 Cl-106 HCO3-30* AnGap-6*

2017-04-18 CXR
The patient is status post CABG with median sternotomy. Right IJ
line remains in place. Left-sided chest tubes and mediastinal
drainage tube has been removed. Again, note is made of
cardiomegaly. Widened superior mediastinum is noted, unchanged
compared to the prior study, probably representing postsurgical
changes. Again, note is made of bibasilar atelectasis. Mild CHF
is noted.


Brief Hospital Course:
Mr. Harrell was admitted to the John Jones Whitney Medical
Center on 2000-11-30 for surgical management of his coronary artery
disease. He was taken to the operating room where he underwent
coronary artery bypass grafting to three vessels.
Postoperatively he was taken to the cardiac surgical intensive
care unit. On postoperative day one, Mr. Harrell Kelly
neurologically intact and was extubated. His pressors were
slowly weaned off. On postoperative day three, he was
transferred to the cardiac surgical step down unit for further
recovery. Mr. Harrell was gently diuresed towards his
preoperative weight. The physical therapy service was consulted
for his postoperative strength and mobility. He had a foley
catheter reinserted into his bladder for urinary retention.
Flomax was started and he was able to successfully void
following removal of his catheter. Beta blockade was started and
titrated for optimal heart rate and blood pressure control. His
drains and epicardial pacing wires were removed per protocol.
Mr. Harrell had a brief run of atrial fibrillation for which his
beta blockade was advanced. He continued to make steady progress
and was discharged to his home on postoperative day five. He
will follow-up with Dr. Ramirez, his cardiologist and his primary
care physician as an outpatient.

Medications on Admission:
Atenolol 25mg Daily
Lipitor 40mg daily
Lisinopril 10mg daily
Fluoxetine 20mg daily
Aspirin 81mg daily
Maprotiline 150mg daily

Discharge Disposition:
Home With Service

Discharge Diagnosis:

Discharge Condition:
Good.

Discharge Instructions:
Follow medications on discharge instructions.
You may not drive for 4 weeks.
You may not lift more than 10 lbs. for 3 months.
You should shower, let water flow over wounds, pat dry with a
towel.

Followup Instructions:
Make an appointment with Dr. Ramirez for 1-2 weeks.
Make an appointment with Dr. Ramirez for 4 weeks.
